paper_content.title: KRAS Mutations and Primary Resistance of Lung Adenocarcinomas to Gefitinib or Erlotinib
paper_content.authors: William Pao, Theresa Y. Wang, Gregory J. Riely, Vincent A. Miller, Qiulu Pan, Marc Ladanyi, Maureen F. Zakowski, Robert T. Heelan, Mark G. Kris, Harold E. Varmus
paper_content.abstract:
Background: Somatic mutations in the gene for the epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) are found in adenocarcinomas of the lung and are associated with sensitivity to the kinase inhibitors gefitinib (Iressa) and erlotinib (Tarceva). Lung adenocarcinomas also harbor activating mutations in the downstream GTPase, KRAS, and mutations in EGFR and KRAS appear to be mutually exclusive.

Methods and Findings: We sought to determine whether mutations in KRAS could be used to further enhance prediction of response to gefitinib or erlotinib. We screened 60 lung adenocarcinomas defined as sensitive or refractory to gefitinib or erlotinib for mutations in EGFR and KRAS. We show that mutations in KRAS are associated with a lack of sensitivity to either drug.

Conclusion: Our results suggest that treatment decisions regarding use of these kinase inhibitors might be improved by determining the mutational status of both EGFR and KRAS.
paper_content.tables:
[
  {
    "table_id": "Table 1",
    "title": "EGFR and KRAS Mutation Status in Lung Adenocarcinomas Sensitive or Refractory to Gefitinib or Erlotinib",
    "headers": ["Drug", "Gene Mutated", "Proportion Sensitive", "Proportion Refractory", "p-Value"],
    "rows": [
      ["Gefitinib", "EGFR", "9/12", "0/12", "0.0034"],
      ["Gefitinib", "KRAS", "0/12", "5/12", "0.0373"],
      ["Erlotinib", "EGFR", "8/10", "0/26", "1.487 × 10^-6"],
      ["Erlotinib", "KRAS", "0/9*", "4/26", "0.5531^b"],
      ["Gefitinib or Erlotinib", "EGFR", "17/22", "0/38", "6.801 × 10^-11"],
      ["Gefitinib or Erlotinib", "KRAS", "0/21*", "9/38", "0.0201"]
    ],
    "caption": "Lung tumors were examined for mutations in EGFR (exons 18-21) and KRAS (exon 2). In gefitinib-treated patients, six EGFR mutations involved exon 19 deletions that lacked the amino acids Leu-Arg-Glu-Ala, and two were exon 21 amino acid substitutions (L858R). A seventh case with an exon 19 deletion (involving 12 nucleotides) was detected by fluorescent capillary electrophoresis only; so exact sequence deletion information was unavailable (see Methods). In erlotinib-treated patients, three EGFR mutations were exon 19 deletions that lacked amino acids Leu-Arg-Glu-Ala, and five were exon 21 amino acid substitutions (L858R). *One erlotinib-sensitive tumor was unavailable for KRAS examination. ^b The incidence of KRAS mutations in this cohort was low, probably because only cases involving bronchioloalveolar carcinoma were tested (see text)."
  },
  {
    "table_id": "Table 2",
    "title": "KRAS Exon 2 Mutations Found in Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancers Refractory to Treatment with Gefitinib or Erlotinib",

      ["54", "Erlotinib", "TGT GGC", "G12C"],
      ["68", "Erlotinib", "GAT GGC", "G12D"],
      ["69", "Erlotinib", "GAT GGC", "G12D"],

    ],

paper_content.genes: EGFR (epidermal growth factor receptor), KRAS (KRAS2), HER2, ERBB family, N-RAS, HRAS
paper_content.variants: KRAS G12C (codon 12, GGT→TGT), KRAS G13C (codon 13, GGC→TGC), KRAS G12D (codon 12, GGT→GAT), KRAS G12S (codon 12, GGT→AGT), KRAS G12V (codon 12, GGT→GTT), EGFR exon 19 deletions (del Leu-Arg-Glu-Ala), EGFR L858R (exon 21, 2573T→G)
paper_content.diseases: Lung adenocarcinoma, Non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC), Bronchioloalveolar carcinoma
paper_content.therapies: Gefitinib (Iressa) - 250 mg or 500 mg orally once daily, Erlotinib (Tarceva) - 150 mg orally once daily
paper_content.clinical_trials: Multicenter Phase II Trial of OSI-774 (Erlotinib, Tarceva) in Patients with Advanced Bronchioloalveolar Cell Lung Cancer (Protocol S2, referenced in Supporting Information)

draft_extractions.evidence_description: KRAS mutations are associated with primary resistance to gefitinib in lung adenocarcinoma patients. The study screened 60 lung adenocarcinomas defined as sensitive or refractory to gefitinib and demonstrated that mutations in KRAS are associated with a lack of sensitivity to gefitinib treatment. The authors conclude that treatment decisions regarding use of gefitinib might be improved by determining the mutational status of KRAS.
draft_extractions.verbatim_quote: We show that mutations in KRAS are associated with a lack of sensitivity to either drug.
draft_extractions.extraction_reasoning: This is the primary finding of the paper. The study directly demonstrates that KRAS mutations predict resistance to gefitinib in lung adenocarcinoma through analysis of 60 patient samples, which is clinically actionable as it informs treatment decisions for EGFR TKI therapy.

draft_extractions.evidence_description: KRAS mutations are associated with primary resistance to erlotinib in lung adenocarcinoma patients. The study screened 60 lung adenocarcinomas defined as sensitive or refractory to erlotinib and demonstrated that mutations in KRAS are associated with a lack of sensitivity to erlotinib treatment. The authors conclude that treatment decisions regarding use of erlotinib might be improved by determining the mutational status of KRAS.

The study directly demonstrates that KRAS mutations predict resistance to erlotinib in lung adenocarcinoma through analysis of 60 patient samples, which is clinically actionable as it informs treatment decisions for EGFR TKI therapy.
